title: update same-version resync verbatim-overwrites the installed schema with no block-item re-validation — a narrowing catalog change silently invalidates existing items
body: update routes a catalog-ahead schema whose body changed at the SAME version through resyncSchema's verbatim-overwrite arm (index.ts:1066): the installed schema file is replaced with the catalog body and no existing block item is re-validated against it. A catalog change that narrows validity at the same version — dropping a property under additionalProperties:false, adding a required field, narrowing an enum — silently leaves existing block items invalid, with no refusal and no warning. This is asymmetric with the version-bump path, which forward-migrates + re-validates the items and refuses (blocked, byte-unchanged) when they would fail. Concretely: the catalog story schema omits user_kind under additionalProperties:false while 14 live story items carry user_kind; update --dryRun classifies that schema resync (not blocked), so applying it invalidates those items.
